currentUse: Seventh-day Adventist church — Getsemani SDA Church, a Spanish-language congregation at 348 Smith St, owned by the Northeastern Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
summary: The former Šv. Kazimiero (St. Casimir) Lithuanian church at 348-350 Smith Street in Providence's Smith Hill neighborhood is still standing and remains in religious use. The Diocese of Providence announced the parish's permanent closure on June 7, 2017, effective July 1, 2017, citing an estimated $90,000 roof repair beyond the parish's means and collapsed sacramental life (no baptisms, first communions, or confirmations 2014-2016; the final Mass on March 19, 2017 drew 12 people); sacramental records went to St. Patrick Parish in Providence. Providence tax rolls show the church parcel (348 Smith St, plat 67 lot 67) owned by the Roman Catholic Bishop of Providence on the 2017 roll and by "The North Eastern Conference [of Seventh-]Day Adventist Church" (headquartered in Jamaica, NY) on the 2018 roll, placing the transfer around late 2017. The building today houses Getsemani SDA Church, a Spanish-language Seventh-day Adventist congregation (pastor listed as Tulio Terrero), and the parcel remains church-classed on the city's 2025 tax roll with a total assessment of $985,200. The adjacent 350 Smith St parcel, church-classed on the 2016 roll, appears on the 2017 and later rolls as a privately owned single-family home. The Global True Lithuania heritage site reports the Lithuanian memorial next to the church, topped with a traditional Lithuanian sun-cross, still stands. No preservation campaign, reunion, or recent press coverage of the former parish was found.

developments.headline: Diocese of Providence announces permanent closure of St. Casimir Parish
developments.detail: The diocese announced on June 7, 2017 that St. Casimir Parish (350 Smith Street) would close permanently effective July 1, 2017, citing significant physical deterioration of the building (roof/ceiling leaks, roof repair estimated at ~$90,000) and severe decline in parish life: no baptisms, first communions, or confirmations 2014-2016, one wedding in that period, and only 12 attendees at the final Mass on March 19, 2017. Sacramental records were transferred to St. Patrick Parish, Providence. The parish had served Lithuanian Catholics since 1919 and occupied the Smith Street church since 1935.

sources.title: St. Casimir Parish in Providence to close permanently

developments.headline: Church property transferred from the diocese to the Northeastern Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
developments.detail: Providence property tax rolls show the church parcel at 348 Smith St (plat 67, lot 67) and the rear parcel 348R (lot 68) owned by the Roman Catholic Bishop of Providence / St. Casimir's Church on the 2016 and 2017 rolls, and by 'The North Eastern Conference Day Adventist Church' (owner address 55 Merrick Blvd, Jamaica, NY — the Northeastern Conference of Seventh-day Adventists) beginning with the 2018 roll, indicating a transfer around late 2017. The parcels remained fully tax-exempt as church property (2020 roll: assessment $942,900, fully exempt). No published sale price was found. The adjacent 350 Smith St parcel (lot 66), church-classed on the 2016 roll, appears from the 2017 roll onward as a privately owned single-family home.

developments.headline: Building in active use as Getsemani SDA Church, a Spanish-language Adventist congregation
developments.detail: The official Adventist church locator (eAdventist) lists Getsemani SDA Church at 348 Smith St, Providence RI 02908-3726 — the former St. Casimir building — offering services in Spanish, with Tulio Terrero listed as pastor. The city's 2025 tax roll still classes 348 Smith St as a church owned by the Northeastern Conference, with a total assessment of $985,200 ($41,300 for the rear parcel). The Lithuanian heritage site Global True Lithuania confirms the 1934-1935 building is used by a non-Catholic denomination and that the Lithuanian memorial with a traditional sun-cross next to the church still stands.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record's core facts are confirmed and refined: (1) closure in 2017 confirmed — announced June 7, 2017, effective July 1, 2017, final Mass March 19, 2017 (turnto10.com); (2) "building sold to Seventh-day Adventists" confirmed via Providence tax rolls — owner changed from Roman Catholic Bishop of Providence (2017 roll) to the Northeastern Conference of Seventh-day Adventists (2018 roll), and the building now houses Getsemani SDA Church, a Spanish-language congregation; (3) refinement: the parish's founding, not stated in the archive, is dated to 1919 by the closure coverage, with the Smith Street church built in 1934-1935. The archive note that the community declined a diocesan offer to bring in Latin American immigrants could not be independently verified, though the building's current occupant is, in fact, a Spanish-language congregation. Minor address refinement: news reports give 350 Smith Street, but the church itself sits on the parcel addressed 348 Smith St (plat 67, lot 67); the 350 Smith St parcel is now a privately owned single-family home (plausibly the former rectory, though its rectory function is unverified).
gaps: Sale price and exact deed date were not published anywhere found — searched news (WPRI, Providence Business News, RI Catholic), real-estate listing sites, and Providence open data (no deeds dataset; the city's Recorder of Deeds records are not web-searchable, and Providence has no Vision/VGSI parcel-card site with sale history). The transfer is bracketed to c. late 2017 by the 2017-vs-2018 tax rolls only. The Diocese of Providence closure announcement page and Rhode Island Catholic's 90th-anniversary article both returned HTTP 403 and could not be read directly. Whether the "Iglesia Adventista Hispana Smith St" Facebook page is the same congregation as Getsemani SDA Church is unconfirmed (the Facebook page shows no address). The fate of the church's Lithuanian interior artifacts is unknown. No press coverage, preservation effort, or parishioner reunion activity after 2017 was found; the American Lithuanian Citizens Beneficial Club in Providence still appears in directories but no verifiable recent activity was located.
